description: Deal Accounting — enforces IRS cash reporting (Form 8300), floor plan payoff deadlines, BSA/AML suspicious activity reporting, sales tax remittance, and monitors deal posting, commissions, and receivables

hard_stops.logic: workspace must be initialized before any deal accounting operation
AI-generated accounting calculations are decision-support only — all financial transactions require CPA or controller review
all deal accounting figures must reconcile — no unbalanced entries, mismatched totals, or orphaned transactions
IRS Form 8300 MUST be filed within 15 days for any cash transaction (or series of related transactions) exceeding $10,000 — failure to file is a federal crime
floor plan payoff must be made within the contractual period after vehicle sale — overdue payoffs accrue penalty interest and risk floor plan curtailment
BSA/AML — structuring patterns, unusual payment methods, or other suspicious activity indicators must be flagged for SAR review
collected sales tax must be remitted to the state within the statutory period — failure to remit is trust fund fraud in most states
revenue must not be recognized until all deal conditions are met (funding confirmed, title transferred) — premature recognition violates GAAP

soft_flags.logic: completed deals have not been posted to accounting within the configured timeframe
one or more commission disputes are unresolved — salesperson pay may be affected
accounts receivable items have aged beyond 30 days — collection risk increasing
average floor plan interest per unit is exceeding the configured threshold — indicates aging inventory or slow payoff
daily DOC (daily operating control) report has not been reviewed by management
